medications.name: Ceftriaxone
medications.indication: Antibiotic prophylaxis for variceal hemorrhage (and SBP prophylaxis)
medications.notes: First antibiotic of the admission, started with the variceal bleed.

medications.name: Vancomycin
medications.indication: Empiric broad-spectrum coverage for presumed sepsis (post-TIPS fevers/hypotension)
medications.instructions: Broadened empirically when she developed fevers and pressor requirement.

medications.name: Meropenem
medications.indication: Empiric broad-spectrum coverage for presumed sepsis
medications.instructions: Broadened empirically with the fevers; continued despite a negative infectious workup.
medications.notes: Renally dose-adjusted given AKI. (Beta-lactams are continued through the prolonged fever course.)

medications.name: Vasopressin
medications.instructions: Added when norepinephrine alone was insufficient.

medications.name: Lactulose
medications.indication: Hepatic encephalopathy (also for post-TIPS encephalopathy risk)
medications.instructions: Titrate to stooling; important given TIPS-associated encephalopathy risk.

_note: Antibiotic course: ceftriaxone (5/2, variceal-bleed prophylaxis) escalated to vancomycin + meropenem (5/4) for presumed sepsis, then continued through a prolonged fever course despite a negative infectious workup. Two pressors and sedation were used during the ICU stay. (How the antibiotics were ultimately implicated is documented in the later hospital course.)
